# Copilot instructions for divine-tarot

Purpose: provide repository-specific guidance for Copilot-powered sessions (build/test/lint commands, architecture overview, and conventions) so assistants can act effectively.

## Quick commands
- Dev server: npm run dev
- Build (includes validations): npm run build
- Start production server: npm run start

Lint & typings
- Full lint: npm run lint
- Auto-fix lint: npm run lint:fix
- Type-check only: npm run typecheck
- Husky pre-commit runs: npm run typecheck && npm run lint

Repository validation & utilities
- Env validation (required before build): npm run validate:env
- i18n generation/checks: npm run i18n:gen and npm run i18n:check (validate:i18n runs during build)
- Dependency/graph checks: npm run graph and npm run graph:check (graph:check detects circular deps)
- Barrel file generation: npm run barrels (barrels:watch for watching changes)
- Project-wide import check: npm run check:imports

Testing
- There are no test scripts or test files in the repository at present. (No npm test script in package.json.)
- If tests are added, follow the repository's npm script convention (add a top-level `test` script). For single-test execution follow the test harness' CLI (e.g., `npx <test-runner> path/to/single.test.ts`).

## High-level architecture (big picture)
- This is a Next.js (app router) application (Next v14) using the `app/` folder for routes and React Server/Client components.
- Source layout (important dirs):
  - app/ — Next.js app routes and pages
  - components/ — shared React UI components
  - config/ — runtime/static configuration
  - lib/ — utilities and integrations (e.g., OpenAI, Supabase wrappers)
  - i18n/ and translations/ — internationalization sources; scripts/ validate/ generate i18n types
  - store/ — client-side state (e.g., zustand)
  - data/ and types/ — domain data shapes and shared type definitions
- Runtime & infra hints:
  - Uses Supabase (server and client packages) and may include server-side auth/SSR paths via @supabase/ssr
  - Integrates with OpenAI SDK and third-party services (Twilio, Razorpay, Resend)
  - Next.js images set to unoptimized in next.config.mjs (images.unoptimized: true)
- Build pipeline:
  - `npm run build` runs environment and i18n validation scripts before the Next build — ensure env and translation validations pass in CI.

## Key repository-specific conventions
- TypeScript path alias: `@/*` resolves to `./src/*` and `./*`. Prefer imports like `@/components/Button`.
- Strict TypeScript: tsconfig.json sets `strict: true` and `noEmit: true` — rely on `npm run typecheck` for type validation.
- Husky pre-commit hook: runs typecheck and lint to catch issues early.
- i18n flow: translations are validated during build (`validate:i18n`) and types are generated by `i18n:gen`. Treat `translations/` as the source of truth for locales.
- Architecture checks: circular dependency detection is enforced by `graph:check`; keep modules layered to avoid cycles.
- Barrel files: barrelsby is used to generate barrel exports under `src` (script `barrels`). Maintain file structure to keep barrels deterministic.
- Lint import-check: `npm run check:imports` runs eslint on `src` with `--max-warnings=0` to enforce clean imports.

## Files & scripts of interest
- package.json — canonical npm scripts and husky hooks
- scripts/validateEnv.js — ensures required env vars exist for build/runtime
- scripts/validateTranslations.js and scripts/gen-i18n-types.ts — i18n tooling
- scripts/design-audit.js and scripts/optimize-cli.ts — project-specific tooling

## Notes for Copilot sessions
- When proposing code changes that affect runtime behavior, ensure `validate:env` and `validate:i18n` still pass (they run on `npm run build`).
- Prefer using existing scripts instead of inventing new commands; add new npm scripts only when necessary and update this file.
- Do not assume tests exist; if adding tests, also add a `test` npm script and document how to run a single test.

If this file should be extended (CI instructions, environment matrix, or MCP server configs), indicate where to add them.
